extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by versus against each entity: I excluded the Company’s “By the Company” customs refund matter of Rs. 499.71 lakhs and the “Against promoters” tax matter of Rs. 34.83 lakhs because they are not company/subsidiary matters; all criminal, statutory/regulatory and company/subsidiary tax cells in the “Against” rows are dashes, while all “By” rows are excluded. The contingent-liabilities figure is the explicitly stated consolidated Total of Rs. 17.00 lakhs as at September 30, 2021, not a re-sum; this converts from lakhs to Rs. 1.70 crore (17.00 ÷ 100). Customer concentration is disclosed for Fiscal 2021, a full fiscal year, as 33.13% for the top ten customers, using revenue from operations; the top-five percentage is derived from the five largest listed customer percentages for that year (3.17% + 2.81% + 2.56% + 1.77% + 1.47% = 11.78%). Litigation and contingent-liability tables use Rs. in lakhs, converted to crore at 100 lakhs per crore.
